message:
fix(unix): inode-safe socket file cleanup prevents accidental unlink of replacement sockets

Previously, UnixListener and UnixDatagram unconditionally removed the socket
file at their bound path on drop. This was dangerous when another process (or
a rebind within the same process) created a new socket at the same path after
the original was unlinked — the old handle's drop would delete the replacement.

Introduce SocketFileIdentity (dev + ino pair) captured at bind time. On drop,
the socket file is only removed if the current file at the path has the same
dev/ino as the one originally bound. If identity capture fails at bind time,
automatic cleanup is skipped entirely rather than risk deleting a different
socket.

New helpers in listener.rs:
- socket_file_identity(): captures dev/ino via symlink_metadata
- remove_socket_file_if_same_inode(): conditional unlink with identity check

Both UnixListener and UnixDatagram gain a cleanup_identity field, and
take_path() now also clears the identity to fully disable cleanup.

Tests verify that dropping the original handle after a replacement socket
has been bound at the same path does not remove the replacement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
